mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 prices within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, driving corporate earnings revisions and generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that erode profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in late-cycle regimes produce positive 20-day forward returns in over 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 2.5 percent year-over-year forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, triggering immediate sell-offs in equity indices.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL surpasses the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate demonstrates robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, lifting equity valuations over three months.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1000 thousand units during a post-shock recovery phase indicates a bottoming construction cycle that precedes policy accommodation and equity market rebounds.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances within post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output that improves short-term earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes fail to predict 5-day forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across out-of-sample tests.
